raw.objective: “to quantify the predictive ability of the WT equation in a larger sample of neuro-intensive care unit (NICU) patients and generate a new equation that may be more predictive”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, SEX, total phenytoin concentration, albumin, CLcr, concomitant medications Included: total phenytoin concentration, albumin, SEX, CLcr
raw.observations: As opposed to Ter Heine et al. (see above), the proposed model should better predict free PHT in critically ill patients specifically
raw.equations: x=-theta1+theta2*TPC+theta3*(TPC**2)+theta4*ALB+theta5*SEX+theta6*CLCR PFP = exp(x) TPC is total phenytoin concentration (mg/L) ALB is serum albumin concentration (g/dL) CLCR is creatinine clearance (Cockcroft-Gault method) PFP is predicted free phenytoin concentration (mg/L)

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to quantify the predictive ability of the WT equation in a larger sample of neuro-intensive care unit (NICU) patients and generate a new equation that may be more predictive”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, SEX, total phenytoin concentration, albumin, CLcr, concomitant medications Included: total phenytoin concentration, albumin, SEX, CLcr

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
